text: The surgeon performs an inventory of the abdominal cavity, visually checking the anastomosis marked in blue. The distal ileum is identified, and a gap is created through the mesentery to transect the terminal ileum, separating it from the surgical specimen.

An endostapler is inserted to transect the ileum near the anastomosis. The surgeon then transects the mesentery of the distal ileum up to the lateral aspect of the cecum, detaching the mesentery from the right colon.
The surgeon begins dissection from lateral to medial due to limited exposure under the ileocecal vessels, proceeding through the Toldt's fascia adjacent to the colon.

The surgeon performs a partial resection of the Gerota's fascia to prevent positive circumferential margins near the tumor. The right kidney is visible, and the resected portion of Gerota's fascia is highlighted in green.
The surgeon continues lateral mobilization, detaching the right colon from surrounding tissues up to the hepatic flexure, ensuring the correct anatomical plane is followed.

The surgeon completes the lateral mobilization of the colon, then proceeds to dissect the gastroepiploic ligament to mobilize the transverse colon. The dissection separates the transverse colon from the gallbladder, approaching the tumor and the region where it invades the liver.

The surgeon performs a medial to lateral dissection, targeting the ileocecal vessels near the superior mesenteric vein. The mesentery is opened along the vein's axis, creating a space to separate the pancreas and duodenum from the specimen. The duodenum and pancreas are now visibly free from the specimen.
The surgeon performs a secure dissection and ligation of the ileocecal vessels using a clip and bipolar advanced energy device, ensuring precise control over the vessels.

The surgeon completes the detachment and mobilization of the duodenum, followed by nearly completing the medial to lateral dissection. The transverse mesocolon is then ligated and treated, indicating progress in separating the transverse colon from surrounding structures.
The surgeon creates a gap to insert the endostapler, transecting the distal margin near the previous anastomosis. The transverse colon is stapled, ensuring a proper distal margin.

The surgeon performs a partial hepatectomy using bipolar advanced energy, sealing and treating the liver parenchyma. Following the hepatectomy, the tumor remains attached to the gyrotous fascia, and medial dissection is carried out to complete the hepatic fracture mobilization and the right colectomy.

The surgeon performs a partial resection of the Gerotas fascia, visible in green. Hemostasis is achieved using monopolar energy, followed by the placement of Surgicel. The specimen is extracted through a mylar incision, and a closed suction drain is inserted near the liver. The extracted specimen is displayed.
